rationale.completeness: The PR fully addresses all requirements from the feature description: multi-ticker input, relative performance metrics, risk metrics (volatility, Sharpe ratio, beta, VaR), and correlation analysis. All 40 tasks are marked complete and cover all 4 user stories plus CLI integration and error handling.
rationale.correctness: Financial formulas are correctly implemented using standard calculations (daily returns, annualized return, Pearson correlation, Sharpe ratio with 4.5% risk-free rate). Edge cases are handled including invalid tickers, insufficient data warnings, and single ticker validation. The math appears sound.
rationale.style: The code follows TypeScript conventions and aligns with existing patterns in src/tools/finance/. Uses Zod for validation as specified in the plan. Module structure is consistent with project organization, though some speckit command files have substantial boilerplate.
rationale.quality: Excellent code organization with clear separation: calculations/, services/, formatters/, types.ts, validation.ts, parser.ts, data-service.ts. Well-structured interfaces, proper error handling, and good abstraction layers. The CLI integration is clean with proper flag parsing and output formatting.
